root_text.an4.13:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu anuppannānaṁ pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ anuppādāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati;
root_text.an4.13:1.4: uppannānaṁ pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati;
root_text.an4.13:1.5: anuppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ uppādāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati;
root_text.an4.13:1.6: uppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ ṭhitiyā asammosāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
root_text.an4.13:2.3: Te tusitā jetvā māraṁ savāhiniṁ te anejā,

translation_text.an4.13:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four right efforts.
translation_text.an4.13:1.3: A mendicant generates enthusiasm, tries, makes an effort, exerts the mind, and strives so that bad, unskillful qualities don’t arise.
translation_text.an4.13:1.4: They generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that bad, unskillful qualities that have arisen are given up.
translation_text.an4.13:1.5: They generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that skillful qualities arise.
translation_text.an4.13:1.6: They generate enthusiasm, try, make an effort, exert the mind, and strive so that skillful qualities that have arisen remain, are not lost, but increase, mature, and are fulfilled by development.
translation_text.an4.13:1.7: These are the four right efforts.
translation_text.an4.13:2.1: By rightly striving, <j>they’ve crushed Māra’s sovereignty;
translation_text.an4.13:2.2: unattached, they’ve gone beyond  <j>the peril of birth and death.
translation_text.an4.13:2.3: Contented and unstirred, they’ve vanquished Māra with his legions;
translation_text.an4.13:2.4: now they’ve gone beyond all Namuci’s forces, <j>they’re happy.”
